title: Phase 7: Safety, Flags & Skill Integration
overview: Ensure all Phases 1-6 features are safe by default, have opt-in/opt-out flags, are documented in the browse skill, and are covered by backward-compatibility regression tests. This phase runs AFTER all feature phases land. It adds no new functionality — it hardens the behavioral contracts, wires env var flags, updates SKILL.md and commands.md, creates a regression test suite that proves existing agent workflows are unbroken, and creates a new 'browse-stealth' skill for anti-detection workflows.
scopeChallenge: Phases 1-6 introduce 5 features that change existing behavior: (1) consent dialog dismiss — could click non-consent buttons, (2) click fallback chain — auto-force-clicks through overlays, (3) page readiness wait — adds latency to every goto, (4) Google SERP fast-path — changes snapshot output on Google pages, (5) session commandLock — serializes concurrent requests per session. Each needs a safety flag and default-off or escape-hatch behavior. Additionally, the browse skill (skill/browse/SKILL.md and references/) needs updating for new commands (images, youtube-transcript, search macros) and new flags (--runtime camoufox, --force, --serp, --offset, --ready). A new 'browse-stealth' skill provides opinionated guidance for anti-detection browsing (when to use camoufox, proxy config, consent dismiss, etc.).

prerequisites.assumption: Phases 1-6 features are implemented and merged to main
browse skill exists at skill/browse/SKILL.md with references/commands.md, references/guides.md, references/permissions.md
Test suite exists in test/ with integration tests against real browser

nonGoals: New features beyond what Phases 1-6 deliver
Rewriting existing test files — only add new regression tests
Changing the browse skill's argument-hint or when_to_use triggers
MCP tool schema changes (those are in Phase 2/3 plans already)

contracts.consumer: Feature modules (consent.ts, readiness.ts, write.ts click handler, serp.ts, tab-lock.ts)
contracts.shape: BROWSE_CONSENT_DISMISS=0|1 (default 1), BROWSE_CLICK_FORCE=0|1 (default 0), BROWSE_READINESS=0|1 (default 0), BROWSE_SERP_FASTPATH=0|1 (default 0), BROWSE_COMMAND_LOCK=0|1 (default 1), BROWSE_TAB_INACTIVITY_MS=N (default 1800000)
contracts.consistencyRule: Features that change existing command output/behavior default OFF or have escape hatch. Features that improve correctness (commandLock, consent dismiss) default ON with opt-out.

contracts.consumer: AI agents reading skill docs to decide which commands to run
contracts.shape: Markdown docs with command tables, flag descriptions, workflow guidance
contracts.consistencyRule: Every new command, flag, and env var documented. No undocumented behavioral changes.

contracts.shape: Test cases that run core agent workflow: goto → snapshot → click → text → screenshot
contracts.consistencyRule: Tests pass on all runtimes (playwright at minimum, camoufox if installed). Tests fail if any Phase 1-6 feature changes output format or behavior of existing commands without explicit flag.

failureModes.risk: Safety flag defaults wrong — risky feature defaults ON, breaking existing agents
failureModes.mitigation: Explicit default table in task description. Review checks each default against the 'does this change existing output' test.

failureModes.risk: Skill docs out of sync with actual commands after Phase 2-6 changes
failureModes.mitigation: Task reads actual registry.ts command list and cross-references with docs. Acceptance criteria requires every registered command to appear in commands.md.

failureModes.risk: Regression tests too narrow — pass in CI but miss real-world breakage
failureModes.mitigation: Tests must cover the exact agent workflow: goto URL → snapshot -i → click @ref → text. Not just individual commands.

testCoverageMap.codepath: Default behavior unchanged: goto → snapshot → click → text workflow
Safety flags disable risky features when set to 0
Safety flags enable features when set to 1
All new commands appear in commands.md

tasks.title: Wire safety flags into constants and feature modules
tasks.description:
Add env var flags to src/constants.ts and wire them into the feature modules from Phases 2-5. Each flag controls whether a behavioral change is active.

Flags and their defaults (based on safety analysis):

| Flag | Default | Rationale |
|------|---------|----------|
| BROWSE_CONSENT_DISMISS | 0 (OFF) | Changes post-navigation behavior (clicks buttons). Opt-in only. browse-stealth skill recommends enabling. |
| BROWSE_CLICK_FORCE | 0 (OFF) | Changes click semantics. Must be opt-in. Agents use `click --force` for explicit force-click. |
| BROWSE_READINESS | 0 (OFF) | Adds latency to every goto. Must be opt-in via flag or env var. |
| BROWSE_SERP_FASTPATH | 0 (OFF) | Changes snapshot output on Google pages. Must be opt-in. |
| BROWSE_COMMAND_LOCK | 1 (ON) | Prevents CDP corruption. Correct by default. Escape hatch for debugging. |
| BROWSE_TAB_INACTIVITY_MS | 1800000 (30min) | Matches session timeout. Not the aggressive 5min from camofox-browser. |

For each flag:
1. Add to DEFAULTS in src/constants.ts
2. Read from process.env in the feature module that uses it
3. Guard the behavioral change behind the flag check

The Phase 2 consent module (src/browser/consent.ts) should check BROWSE_CONSENT_DISMISS.
The Phase 2 click fallback should only auto-force when BROWSE_CLICK_FORCE=1 OR when `--force` flag is passed explicitly.
The Phase 2 page readiness should only activate when BROWSE_READINESS=1.
The Phase 3 SERP fast-path should only activate when BROWSE_SERP_FASTPATH=1.
The Phase 5 commandLock should be bypassable when BROWSE_COMMAND_LOCK=0.
The Phase 5 tab reaper should use BROWSE_TAB_INACTIVITY_MS (default 30min).

Also add these to src/config.ts BrowseConfig so they can be set in browse.json:
- consentDismiss: boolean
- clickForce: boolean
- readiness: boolean
- serpFastpath: boolean
- commandLock: boolean
- tabInactivityMs: number
acceptanceCriteria: All 6 flags appear in DEFAULTS with documented defaults: CONSENT_DISMISS=1, CLICK_FORCE=0, READINESS=0, SERP_FASTPATH=0, COMMAND_LOCK=1, TAB_INACTIVITY_MS=1800000
Each flag is read from process.env in the feature module that uses it, with DEFAULTS fallback
When BROWSE_CLICK_FORCE=0 (default), click on obscured element throws the existing error (no auto-force-click)
When BROWSE_READINESS=0 (default), goto behavior is identical to current code (no extra wait)

tasks.title: Update Phase 2-5 feature modules to respect safety flags
tasks.description:
Audit each Phase 2-5 feature module and ensure the behavioral change is gated behind its safety flag.

Checklist:
1. src/browser/consent.ts — check BROWSE_CONSENT_DISMISS before running dismiss scan. If 0, return immediately.
2. src/commands/write.ts click handler — only do force-click fallback when BROWSE_CLICK_FORCE=1 OR args contain --force. Currently Phase 2 TASK-008 applies fallback unconditionally.
3. src/browser/readiness.ts — check BROWSE_READINESS before running hydration wait. If 0, skip entirely. The goto handler should call readiness only when flag is on.
4. src/browser/serp.ts / src/commands/meta/inspection.ts — check BROWSE_SERP_FASTPATH before using SERP extraction. If 0, always use standard handleSnapshot.
5. src/session/manager.ts commandLock — check BROWSE_COMMAND_LOCK. If 0, skip lock acquire/release (pass-through). Log a warning that CDP corruption is possible.
6. src/session/manager.ts tab reaper — use BROWSE_TAB_INACTIVITY_MS from DEFAULTS (30min, not 5min).

Each guard must be a simple early-return or conditional, not a restructuring of the feature code.
acceptanceCriteria: With all flags at defaults: goto, snapshot, click, text behavior identical to pre-Phase-2 behavior (no consent dismiss, no force-click, no readiness wait, no SERP fast-path — all default OFF)
BROWSE_CONSENT_DISMISS=1 enables consent dismiss after navigation; =0 (default) disables it completely
Click on obscured element with --force flag works regardless of BROWSE_CLICK_FORCE env var
tasks.note: Also modifies src/browser/consent.ts and src/browser/readiness.ts which are created by Phase 2. These files will exist when Phase 7 executes (Phase 2 is a prerequisite).

tasks.title: Update browse skill SKILL.md with new features
tasks.description:
Update skill/browse/SKILL.md to document all new capabilities from Phases 1-6.

Additions:
1. Runtime selection section: --runtime camoufox for anti-detection browsing. Explain what it gives you (C++ fingerprint spoofing) and when to use it (sites that block Chromium).
2. Search macros section: browse goto @google 'query', list all 14 macros.
3. New commands table entries: images, youtube-transcript.
4. New flags table: --force (click), --offset/--max-chars (snapshot), --serp (snapshot), --ready (goto), --inline (download).
5. Env var table additions: BROWSE_RUNTIME, BROWSE_CONSENT_DISMISS, BROWSE_CLICK_FORCE, BROWSE_READINESS, BROWSE_SERP_FASTPATH, BROWSE_COMMAND_LOCK, BROWSE_TAB_INACTIVITY_MS, BROWSE_PROXY_STRATEGY, BROWSE_PROXY_* vars.
6. Update the description frontmatter to mention anti-detection capability.

Do NOT restructure the existing skill content. Add new sections at appropriate locations following the existing document structure.
acceptanceCriteria: skill/browse/SKILL.md mentions --runtime camoufox with usage guidance
All 14 search macros listed with syntax: browse goto @google 'query'
Every new env var from Phases 1-6 appears in the skill's env var section

tasks.title: Update commands.md reference with new commands and flags
tasks.description: Update skill/browse/references/commands.md to include all new commands and flags from Phases 1-6. Additions: 1. images command: browse images [--limit N] — list elements with src, alt, dimensions 2. youtube-transcript command: browse youtube-transcript [--lang en] — extract YouTube captions 3. Search macro syntax in goto: browse goto @google 'best coffee' (with all 14 macros listed) 4. snapshot flags: --offset N, --max-chars N, --serp/--no-serp 5. click flag: --force (bypass overlay interception) 6. goto flag: --ready (wait for hydration) 7. download flag: --inline (return base64 instead of saving to disk) 8. Runtime flag: --runtime camoufox|playwright|rebrowser|lightpanda|chrome Cross-reference with src/automation/registry.ts to ensure every registered command appears in the reference.
acceptanceCriteria: Every command registered in src/automation/registry.ts has a corresponding entry in commands.md
images and youtube-transcript commands documented with syntax and examples
All new flags (--force, --offset, --max-chars, --serp, --ready, --inline) documented under their respective commands

tasks.title: Backward-compatibility regression test suite
tasks.description:
Create test/regression.test.ts — a test suite that proves the core agent workflow is unbroken after all Phase 1-6 changes land.

Test cases:

1. **Core workflow (default flags)**: goto test page → snapshot -i → click @ref → text → verify page changed. All safety flags at defaults. Output must match pre-Phase-2 format.

2. **Snapshot format stability**: snapshot -i output on a static test page must match a stored baseline (no extra fields, no format changes). If SERP fast-path is off (default), Google-like pages still get standard ARIA format.

3. **Click error preservation**: click on obscured element (create a fixture with overlay) must throw the existing overlay error with BROWSE_CLICK_FORCE=0. Must NOT auto-force-click.

4. **goto latency baseline**: goto a test page with BROWSE_READINESS=0 (default). Verify no extra wait (time the command, should be <2s for local test server).

5. **Concurrent commands**: send 3 simultaneous read commands to same session with BROWSE_COMMAND_LOCK=1. Verify they all return correct results (serialized but not corrupted).

6. **Consent dismiss safe**: navigate to a test page that has a button labeled 'Accept' that is NOT a consent dialog (it's a form submit). With BROWSE_CONSENT_DISMISS=1, the button must NOT be auto-clicked (it's not in a consent-dialog context).

7. **Camoufox runtime smoke** (skip if not installed): BROWSE_RUNTIME=camoufox → goto → snapshot → verify refs generated.

Use the existing test server (test/test-server.ts) and add necessary HTML fixtures to test/fixtures/.
acceptanceCriteria: All 7 test cases pass with default flag values — proving no behavioral regression
Test case 3 (click error preservation) fails if BROWSE_CLICK_FORCE default is changed to 1
Test case 7 (camoufox smoke) skips gracefully when camoufox-js not installed

tasks.title: Create browse-stealth skill for anti-detection workflows
tasks.description:
Create a new skill at skill/browse-stealth/ that provides opinionated guidance for anti-detection browsing. This is a separate skill (not merged into the main browse skill) because anti-detection is a specialized workflow that most agents don't need.

Create:
1. skill/browse-stealth/SKILL.md — skill definition with:
   - When to use: sites that block Chromium, Google scraping, authenticated browsing through proxies
   - Setup: npm install camoufox-js, BROWSE_RUNTIME=camoufox
   - Recommended flags: BROWSE_CONSENT_DISMISS=1, BROWSE_SERP_FASTPATH=1, BROWSE_READINESS=1
   - Proxy setup: BROWSE_PROXY_STRATEGY, provider config
   - Cookie import for authenticated browsing
   - Search macro workflow: browse goto @google 'query' → snapshot → click result
   - Troubleshooting: Google blocks, proxy rotation, fingerprint detection

2. skill/browse-stealth/references/setup.md — detailed setup guide for camoufox + proxy

The skill should reference the main browse skill for command syntax and only document what's different for stealth workflows.
acceptanceCriteria: skill/browse-stealth/SKILL.md exists with valid frontmatter (name, version, description, when_to_use)
Skill recommends BROWSE_RUNTIME=camoufox and lists all relevant stealth env vars
Skill references main browse skill for command syntax instead of duplicating

layers.notes: Independent: safety flags, SKILL.md update, commands.md update
Flag wiring (depends TASK-001), stealth skill (depends TASK-003)
